activities_projects.title: Reflecting on Kindness
activities_projects.objective: Encourage students to analyse the value of kindness in everyday life
steps: Think about a situation where you helped someone in need
Describe the situation briefly
Explain how your action affected the other person
Discuss how small acts of kindness can influence society
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop empathy and understand the social impact of helping others
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of reflection and reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary and Character Traits
activities_projects.objective: Analyse descriptive words used in the poem
steps: Read the words describing the old woman and the schoolboys
Group the words according to personality traits
Explain how these words help readers imagine the characters
Share your observations with the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the role of descriptive vocabulary in character development
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of word grouping and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Understanding Poetic Meaning
activities_projects.objective: Interpret the meaning of important words and phrases in context
steps: Read the lines describing the old woman as 'ragged and grey'
Analyse what these words reveal about her condition
Explain how the weather and environment increase her difficulty
Write your interpretation in the notebook
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students interpret poetic language and context

activities_projects.title: Sequencing Events in the Poem
activities_projects.objective: Develop comprehension by analysing the narrative flow
steps: Identify the key events described in the poem
Arrange the events in chronological order
Explain why the poet structured the poem in this way
Discuss how the sequence builds the message of the poem
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand narrative structure in poetry

activities_projects.title: Exploring the Theme of Empathy
activities_projects.objective: Understand the central theme of empathy and humanity
steps: Identify the main message of the poem
Explain why the other boys ignored the old woman
Analyse why the young boy chose to help her
Write a paragraph explaining the importance of empathy in society
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect literature with real-life moral values

activities_projects.title: Poetic Devices Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Identify literary devices used in the poem
steps: Read the poem carefully
Find examples of simile, alliteration, and repetition
Explain how each device contributes to the poem’s meaning
Present your findings in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how literary devices enhance poetry

activities_projects.title: Imagery Exploration
activities_projects.objective: Analyse imagery used in the poem
steps: Identify lines that describe the street and winter weather
Explain how the poet uses imagery to create a visual scene
Describe how this imagery reflects the old woman’s struggle
Write your interpretation in the notebook
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how imagery creates emotional impact

activities_projects.title: Character Analysis Activity
activities_projects.objective: Compare the behaviour of the characters in the poem
steps: Describe the character of the old woman
Describe the character of the young boy
Compare the behaviour of the boy with that of the other children
Explain what lesson the poet wants readers to learn
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyse character behaviour and moral lessons
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of comparison and reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Group Discussion on Humanity
activities_projects.objective: Develop communication and critical thinking skills
steps: Form groups of four students
Discuss why people sometimes ignore those who need help
Suggest ways students can show kindness in everyday life
Present your group’s ideas to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop teamwork and social awareness
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and quality of ideas (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Diary Entry Writing
activities_projects.objective: Encourage reflective writing about helping others
steps: Imagine you are the boy in the poem
Write a diary entry describing the incident of helping the old woman
Explain what you felt before and after helping her
Conclude by writing what you learned from the experience
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop creative and reflective writing skills
